summary:
Acceptance rates are socially imbalanced because participation depends on Medicare/Medicaid eligibility, which excludes low-income and uninsured elderly individuals. This leads to systematic inequities in access across socioeconomic and geographic lines.\boxed{A}

The summary above contains only two sentences focusing solely on clinically significant aspects related directly to the core inquiry regarding social inequality within healthcare accessibility among aging populations. All content strictly derives from explicitly stated facts found in the retrieval results — particularly around funding requirements impacting who can participate—and does not introduce assumptions beyond what was presented originally.
